description_long: This object is a vintage Philips radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet and a classic mid-century design. The front features a large beige speaker grille above a black control panel, which is equipped with two prominent white tuning knobs and a row of white rectangular push buttons. The control panel also displays a frequency indicator with various markings and the Philips logo. The sides and top are made of polished wood, giving the radio a sturdy and elegant appearance. The back panel is perforated for ventilation and includes a visible antenna, as well as manufacturer information and connection ports. The radio stands on four short black feet, elevating it slightly above the surface.
description: A vintage Philips radio receiver with a wooden cabinet, beige speaker grille, and black control panel featuring white knobs and buttons. The back has a perforated panel and visible antenna.
description_view_0: The front view shows the beige speaker grille, black control panel with two white knobs, a row of white push buttons, and the Philips logo.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the smooth, polished wooden surface with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a perforated back panel, a visible antenna, manufacturer information, and connection ports.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the plain wooden surface, similar to the left side, with no controls or distinguishing features.
